key_facts: A read-only lock on an Azure Kubernetes Service (AKS) cluster limits how you can access cluster resources through the portal.
A read-only lock on a resource group that contains an App Service plan prevents you from scaling up or out of the plan .
A cannot-delete lock on a resource group prevents Resource Manager from automatically deleting deployments in the history.

New-AzResourceLock -LockLevel CanNotDelete -LockName LockSite -ResourceName examplesite -ResourceType Microsoft.Web/sites -ResourceGroupName exampleresourcegroup
 
 To lock a resource group, provide the resource group name.
In the portal, these locks are called Delete and Read-only .
In the command line, these locks are called CanNotDelete and ReadOnly .
